title: Testing, Red Teaming, Release, Rollback, and Production Defense · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved trace contains a plausible production RAG release and defense dossier with one ingestion, normalization, chunking, lexical, embedding, index, filter, fusion, reranking, context, generation, citation, authorization, latency, or recovery defect; identify the first failed stage before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in production RAG release and defense dossier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.
Red team poisoning, indirect injection, embedding manipulation, tenant leaks, secret extraction, unsafe output, tool effects, denial of service, deletion, and incident response.
Verify keyboard, landmarks, headings, focus, status, errors, citations, tables, text alternatives, touch, reduced motion, responsive layouts, comprehension, and escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-production-defense-1ei05t" that uses case 7979 to gate release on component, end-to-end, security, accessibility, load, rollback, and recovery evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Production Defense, keep Rag Production Defense connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-production-defense-1ei05t
# Competency: rag-production-defense.
# Case 7979: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def evidence_rag_production_defense_1ei05t(unit: bool = True, retrieval_eval: bool = True, generation_eval: bool = True, security: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, load: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ei05t = "1ei05t-t50ie1-7979"
    assert evidence_variant_1ei05t
    evidence_axes_1ei05t = (
        "corpus1ei05t",
        "queryt50ie1",
        "tenant7979",
        "index1ei05t7979",
        "promptt50ie17979",
        "evaluation79791ei05t",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ei05t)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"
